title: Implement step — structured_output (M1 SHAPE, grammar-constrained)
description: Implement turns Test's RED into GREEN: writes code per Spec architecture (or per Decide record/plan/design on Decide-only chains), runs firebat after each change, runs emberdeck validate_code_links to detect card↔code drift, and produces atomic commits. TARGET RUNTIME: grammar-constrained generation — this schema is enforced at TOKEN GENERATION (M1 = SHAPE; the model cannot emit a wrong shape/enum/missing-required field). Truth-bearing fields (firebat counts ↔ raw_stdout, emberdeck_drift==0, atomic-commit one-logical-change, declared_next_step chain) are closed by SIBLING M2 validator_contracts listed in x-validator-contracts, NOT by grammar. §5 STORAGE LAW: cross-step refs in based_on are Postgres RowRefs, never file paths. Two file-path kinds survive here and are NOT RowRefs: (a) changes[].file_path and commits[].files[] are git-versioned CODE paths (DECISIONS §5 L66: code lives in git files, not Postgres rows); (b) there is no .blazewrit/* artifact path — the legacy file-first model is dead. firebat and emberdeck are MCP-backed, optional + graceful-degrade (DECISIONS §8), so firebat_results and emberdeck_drift are M3 degradable measurements (Measured | Omitted), never bare values. R23: every measured count (firebat blockers/warnings, emberdeck drift, new_commits_count, changed_files_count) is a CountClaim, never a bare integer. P0 emergency mode (flow_type=bugfix-p0): the Test bypass is a FLAG (emergency_mode), not a dropped field — Implement still emits the full shape; emergency_mode.test_bypass records that Test was deferred to post-Verify retroactive queueing.

result.description: P1 first-class result discriminant (reuses the Investigate compatibility_verdict pattern — success is a declared enum branch, not just failure). implemented = changes written, GATEs (firebat blockers=0 AND emberdeck_drift=0, both measured) passed, commit done. blocked = a GATE blocked (firebat blockers>0 or emberdeck_drift≠0 unresolved by 3x self-converge loop), or commit structurally failed, or a precondition violation / GATE-tool-absent escalate. needs_setup_recovery = setup (deps/infra) failed and auto-rollback restored a clean state — code phase never entered. no_op = LEGITIMATELY no code to change (requested change already exists = real GREEN, or a Decide-only chain whose decision is 'no code change'; principle 3 empty-legal, distinct from a missing/malformed-input escalate). The orchestrator routing table keys off this field; escalate branches (result != implemented) additionally carry failure_origin/escalate_reason/escalate_evidence, and no_op carries no_op_details.

changes.description: Every file the implementation touched, with the kind of change. file_path is a git-versioned CODE path (DECISIONS §5 L66 — code stays a file, NOT a Postgres RowRef and NOT a .blazewrit/* artifact). change_type is a closed enum (grammar-enforced). This array is the ground truth the emberdeck card↔code drift check and the firebat scan run against. minItems:0 — legitimate zero-change results (result=no_op, result=needs_setup_recovery, and pre-code blocked branches: precondition violations, setup-dirty, GATE-tool-absent) emit an empty array rather than fabricating a change; minItems:1 is gated on result=='implemented' via the allOf conditional below.

file_path.description: Repository-relative path of the changed code file (git-versioned, NOT a RowRef).

change_type.description: create = new file, modify = edited existing file, delete = removed file. Closed enum so the model cannot invent a fourth verb.

commits.description: Atomic commits produced — one commit = one logical change (implement-reviewer asserts atomicity). sha is the git commit hash; files[] are git-versioned CODE paths (NOT RowRefs). x-validator-contract (M2): re-resolve each sha in the repo; assert commits[].files == git-recorded files of that commit; assert atomic-commit policy (one logical change per commit) — grammar cannot judge 'logical'.

sha.description: Git commit hash (full or abbreviated). M2 re-resolves it in the repo — a sha that does not exist is a fabricated commit.

message.description: Commit message for this logical unit.

files.description: Code files included in this commit (git-versioned paths, NOT RowRefs). M2 asserts these match git's recorded file list for the sha.

firebat_results.description: Result of the firebat code-quality scan run after the changes. firebat is MCP-backed and optional + graceful-degrade (DECISIONS §8) — so this is an M3 DegradableMeasurement, NOT a bare object. MEASURED branch: value carries blockers and warnings, EACH a CountClaim (R23 — a bare integer for a measured count is the exact hallucination R23 kills; M2 re-parses raw_stdout and re-derives the count). OMITTED branch (R22): firebat absent/failed/timed out — first-class absence, never null, never a '# OMITTED' string, never over-typed to fake-zero blockers. implement-reviewer requires firebat blockers=0; when firebat is Omitted, that gate is delegated to Verify Pass1 (which has its own firebat degrade policy).

blockers.description: Count of firebat blocker findings (R23 CountClaim). implement-reviewer requires this == 0 (M2 asserts value==0 from re-parsed raw_stdout).

warnings.description: Count of firebat warning findings (R23 CountClaim). Non-blocking; M2 re-derives value from raw_stdout.

emberdeck_drift.description: emberdeck validate_code_links result: card↔code drift count. Prose FORCES this to 0 ('emberdeck_drift: 0  # 강제') — but the contract is enforced by an M2 VALIDATOR, not by a schema const, because emberdeck is MCP-backed and degradable (DECISIONS §8). MEASURED branch: value is a CountClaim whose count M2 re-parses and asserts == 0 (drift != 0 fails implement-reviewer). OMITTED branch (R22): emberdeck unavailable/failed — first-class absence; the drift gate is then delegated to Verify Pass1. Modeling the forced-0 as const would make absence inexpressible and force an R22 placeholder; the degradable union + M2 ==0 check is the faithful translation.

value.description: card↔code drift count (R23 CountClaim). M2 forces value==0 — drift != 0 = implement-reviewer FAIL.

new_commits_count.description: Number of new commits this Implement run created (R23 CountClaim — a measured count, never a bare integer). M2 re-parses source.raw_stdout (e.g. 'git rev-list --count HEAD ^<base>') and asserts value is derivable AND value == commits.length.

changed_files_count.description: Number of files the changes[] array touched (R23 CountClaim — measured, never bare). M2 re-parses source.raw_stdout (e.g. 'git diff --name-only <base>..HEAD | wc -l') and asserts value is derivable AND value == changes.length.

emergency_mode.description: P0 emergency-mode FLAG (README §P0). When flow_type=bugfix-p0, Implement runs emergency mode: Test bypassed (Test runs Verify-retroactively), shallow forced, fast fix prioritized, post-stabilization follow-up auto-queued after Verify PASS. CRITICAL: the Test bypass is a FLAG here, NOT a dropped field — Implement still emits the full output shape; emergency_mode records that the bypass happened so it is auditable and so the orchestrator can queue the retroactive Test follow-up. On all non-P0 flows, active=false. x-validator-contract (M2): active=true => orchestrator's flow_type=='bugfix-p0' AND depth=='shallow' AND retroactive_test_followup_queued is honored after Verify PASS.

active.description: true only on flow_type=bugfix-p0. Marks this run as emergency mode.

test_bypass.description: true = Test step was bypassed (deferred to Verify-retroactive). Only meaningful when active=true. M2: active=true => this records the Test deferral; orchestrator queues retroactive Test after Verify PASS.

retroactive_test_followup_queued.description: true = the post-stabilization follow-up (add Test + normal Bug Fix flow) was queued. Set after Verify PASS path. Only meaningful when active=true.

based_on.description: §5 cross-step references — ALL RowRefs (Postgres step_runs row ids), NEVER file paths. INPUT CONTRACT (README L18): minimum 1 of (spec_ref | decide_ref) — exactly one required upstream producer; Spec on Spec-chains, Decide on Decide-only chains (Bug Fix / Chore / Release / Spike / P0). test_ref required only when the flow chain names a Test step (M2/reviewer checks per chain). investigate_ref and ground_ref are always-present enrichment when those steps ran. The anyOf below makes grammar enforce 'at least Spec OR Decide is present'. (anyOf = at-least-one, so a Decide-then-Spec chain carrying BOTH refs is valid; oneOf would wrongly reject it.)

spec_ref.description: RowRef to the Spec step_run (acceptance_criteria, code_architecture, tasks). Present on Spec-chains.

decide_ref.description: RowRef to the Decide step_run (decision_record / option_selection / design_document). Present on Decide-only chains.

test_ref.description: RowRef to the Test step_run (failing RED tests). Required only when the flow chain names a Test step (Bug Fix Test, Refactor with coverage gap, etc.).

investigate_ref.description: RowRef to the Investigate step_run (constraints, risk_surface) — always-present enrichment when Investigate ran.

ground_ref.description: RowRef to the Ground step_run (volatile_state, task_subgraph) — always-present enrichment when Ground ran.

depth.description: Adaptive Step Depth the run executed at (README Step Depth Policy). shallow = minimal patch (single concern); deep = full implementation + setup + multiple commits. Deepen triggers: flow_type ∈ {Feature, Migration, Performance, Compound} | Spec.tasks.length ≥ 3 | Spec.code_architecture.files.length ≥ 5. P0 emergency mode forces shallow (M2: emergency_mode.active=true => depth=='shallow').

unverified.description: R13 floor field (KEEP polarity — DESIGNED to persist; Verify is the single gate). Set true when Implement cannot itself verify GREEN — which is the COMMON case (HARNESS_FLOW_REVIEW L61: Implement self-validation claims 'all tests pass GREEN' but has NO mechanical execution means → that proof is delegated to Verify; and on Decide-only chains with no Test, the GREEN basis is weak). NO 'no unverified remain' forbid is added here. Whether unverified:false is honest is the irreducible self-asserted-truth floor.

declared_next_step.description: R16 advisory declaration of the successor step (typically verify). The producer does NOT free-fill the authoritative value — the orchestrator INJECTS expected_next_step. x-validator-contract (M2, folded into R27): assert declared_next_step == orchestrator's expected_next_step; mismatch = chain divergence.

failure_origin.description: Escalate-branch routing field (shared FailureOrigin enum: implement|spec|decide|test|ground|investigate|cap_exceeded). REQUIRED whenever result != 'implemented' (blocked / needs_setup_recovery / cap_exceeded branches — see the allOf conditional below). principle 2/3: Implement detects an upstream defect and routes via failure_origin (it does NOT emit request_upstream_deepen, which is Decide-only). cap_exceeded = step budget exhausted → orchestrator-level halt+escalate (no auto Implement⇄Reviewer reentry).

escalate_reason.description: Human-readable reason for the escalate branch (GATE 3x-fail residual blockers, GATE-tool-absent, setup-dirty-unrecoverable, commit conflict, budget exhausted, precondition violation). Populated whenever result != 'implemented' (see allOf).

escalate_evidence.description: Evidence backing the escalate (a RowRef, a file:line, firebat/emberdeck raw_stdout ref, git status, or progress summary). Populated whenever result != 'implemented' (see allOf).

no_op_details.description: REQUIRED when result=='no_op' (see allOf). Records WHY there was legitimately no code to change (principle 3 empty-legal, NOT a missing/malformed-input escalate). reason = why no change; evidence = proof (e.g. existing code already GREEN, or the Decide decision mandating zero change); current_state/target_state = the observed-equals-target situation; suggested_action = what the orchestrator/Reflect should do.

allOf.$comment: P1: result=='implemented' requires at least one change; the success branch cannot be empty. Zero-change is reserved for no_op / needs_setup_recovery / pre-code blocked branches.
principle 2/3: every escalate branch (result != implemented) must carry failure_origin/escalate_reason/escalate_evidence so the orchestrator can route. implemented does not.
result=no_op carries its mandated no_op_details (legitimate empty result, principle 3).

x-validator-contracts.check: M2 REQUIRED: re-hash firebat_results.value.blockers.source.raw_stdout to confirm raw_stdout_sha256, re-parse it, assert blockers.value is derivable. implement-reviewer additionally requires blockers.value == 0 (firebat blockers=0). Grammar gives shape; only code makes the count trustworthy.
M2 REQUIRED: re-hash + re-parse firebat_results.value.warnings.source.raw_stdout, assert warnings.value is derivable. Self-asserted count is the R23 residual.

M2 REQUIRED: re-hash + re-parse emberdeck_drift.value.source.raw_stdout, assert value is derivable, AND assert value == 0 (README 'emberdeck_drift: 0  # 강제'). drift != 0 = implement-reviewer FAIL. Modeled as Measured|Omitted (not const 0) because emberdeck is degradable; the ==0 contract lives in this code check, not in grammar. When Omitted, the drift gate is delegated to Verify Pass1.
M2 REQUIRED: re-hash + re-parse new_commits_count.source.raw_stdout (e.g. git rev-list --count), assert value derivable AND value == commits.length.
M2 REQUIRED: re-hash + re-parse changed_files_count.source.raw_stdout (e.g. git diff --name-only | wc -l), assert value derivable AND value == changes.length (deduped by file_path).

M2: for each commit, re-resolve sha in the repo (fabricated sha = reject); assert commits[].files == git's recorded file list for that sha; assert atomic-commit policy (one logical change per commit) — grammar cannot judge 'logical', the reviewer/validator does.
M2: assert every changes[].file_path with its change_type matches git's actual diff status against the base (create/modify/delete) — a claimed change with no diff is hallucinated.

M2: assert min 1 of (spec_ref|decide_ref) present (grammar's oneOf gives shape; code resolves the rows). Each RowRef must resolve to an existing step_runs row of the right step_name. test_ref required iff the flow chain names a Test step (reviewer per chain).

M2: emergency_mode.active=true => orchestrator flow_type=='bugfix-p0' AND depth=='shallow' AND test_bypass=true; retroactive_test_followup_queued honored only after Verify PASS. active=false on all non-P0 flows.
M2: declared_next_step == orchestrator-injected expected_next_step; mismatch = chain divergence (dual-SoT gap).

NO forbid. unverified is designed to persist down the chain (enforcement.md L41); Verify is the single gate that resolves it. Whether unverified:false is honest is the irreducible self-asserted-truth floor — mitigated by Verify re-execution, not eliminated.
